DEFENDANT CORD INVESTMENT COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDANT PSYCHIATRIC EVALUATION WITH DR. MARK STRASSBERG IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE PSYCHIATRIC EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF BY DR. STRASSBERG SHALL CONSIST OF A THREE HOUR ORAL EXAMINATION. RULING - Judge Goldsmith discloses that Philip M. Andersen of Philip M. Andersen & Associates has served as a Discovery Pro Tem Judge in Department 302. This will not affect Judge Goldsmith's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CAROLYN BENGTSON's Motion To Disqualify Counsel Or In The Alternative To Exclude Evidence Or Argument Related To Prior Injuries, Monetary Sanctions Ot $7650.00 Against Attoneys For Defendants And Their Clients And Other Sanctions is Denied. Neither Mr. Silver and his firm Philip M. Andersen & Associates nor Ms. Emmet and her firm Bledsoe Cathcart Diestel Pedersen & Treppa LLP engaged in any unethical or sanctionable behavior to warrant disqualification. The subject records were not illegally obtained and or part of sort of poisonous fruit. Ms. Emmett did not actively solicit any records from Ms. Ginn. Further, the Rhodes records from 2007 to 2009 are not privileged under California Evidence Code section 996; inquiry into the injury that Plaintiff herself as put at-issue is permissible and expressly not privileged under section 996. Mr. Silver's questions at Dr. Rhodes deposition were not improper. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
